controls.title: Account Management
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: iac, supply-chain.
controls.narrative: Application and service accounts (CI/CD service accounts, registry pull/push identities, runtime service accounts) are defined, assigned to roles, provisioned with least privilege, and reviewed. GitHub org/repo membership and team-based access (CODEOWNERS) govern who can change code, workflows, and compliance content; short-lived OIDC tokens are preferred over long-lived secrets. Platform/IdP account management is inherited from the RPOC/CSP.

controls.title: Access Enforcement
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: iac.
controls.narrative: Approved authorizations are enforced: GitHub branch protection + required Code Owner reviews gate changes; RBAC and Kubernetes Pod Security admission ("restricted") gate workloads; Conftest/OPA Rego policy rejects non-conformant IaC; cloud IAM (inherited) gates infrastructure.

controls.title: Least Privilege
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: container, iac.
controls.narrative: Least privilege is enforced throughout: containers run as a non-root user with all Linux capabilities dropped, no privilege escalation, and a read-only root filesystem (deploy/k8s, enforced by Conftest/OPA and verified by the container gate); CI tokens are scoped per job; workflow `permissions:` blocks are minimized; Dockerfiles must set a non-root USER.

controls.title: Literacy Training and Awareness
controls.test_result_rationale: Provide training completion records.
controls.narrative: (PROGRAM TO COMPLETE) Role-based security awareness and secure-development training is assigned and tracked for developers, ISSM/ISSO, and operators (SSDF PO.2.2). Record completion evidence (LMS exports) and reference it here. The pipeline itself contributes a "security champions"/policy-as-code culture but does not substitute for training records.

controls.title: Event Logging
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: iac.
controls.narrative: Auditable events are defined for the application (request/error logging to stdout, captured by the platform), the CI/CD pipeline (GitHub audit log of merges, workflow runs, deployments, access changes), and the registry (push/pull). Platform/orchestrator/cloud audit-event definitions are inherited.

controls.title: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting
controls.test_result_rationale: Manual assessment result from the control catalog.
controls.narrative: (PROGRAM/PLATFORM TO COMPLETE) Audit records (application logs, CI/CD audit log, registry logs) are shipped to the platform's centralized logging/SIEM and reviewed for indicators of compromise per the ConMon strategy; anomalies are reported to the ISSM/AO. Reference the SIEM dashboards/alerts and review cadence here. (Active analysis/response also supports the cATO "active cyber defense" pillar — see crosswalks/cato-evaluation-crosswalk.md.)

controls.title: Audit Record Generation
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: body-of-evidence, iac.
controls.narrative: The application emits structured access/error logs; the CI/CD platform generates audit records for security-relevant events (workflow runs, approvals, secret access, deployments); the registry and orchestrator generate audit records (inherited/common). Pipeline run logs and the consolidated evidence index provide a tamper-evident record of every assessment.

controls.title: Control Assessments
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: body-of-evidence, build-test, container, dast, iac, license, sast, sca, secrets, stig.
controls.narrative: Controls are assessed continuously by the DevSecOps pipeline (SAST, SCA, SBOM, secrets, IaC, container scan, DAST, STIG/SCAP, license, supply-chain) plus the developer-testing gate, with results normalized, mapped to controls, and rolled up into controls.json / the dashboard / the eMASS package on every run. The independent Security Control Assessor (SCA) reviews this evidence, performs any additional Examine/Interview/Test activities, and signs the Security Assessment Report (SAR). For RAISE, the SCA reviews the deltas since the last quarterly review.

controls.title: Plan of Action and Milestones
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: body-of-evidence.
controls.narrative: A POA&M is auto-generated each pipeline run from open findings above the policy threshold (policy/thresholds.yaml), in the eMASS column layout (evidence/boe/poam.csv), with scheduled completion dates computed from the remediation SLA (RAISE: raw High+ in production = 21 calendar days), milestones, source, severity (CAT), residual risk, and overdue/out-of-RAISE- scope flags. Human-managed POA&M items (risk acceptances, process weaknesses, inherited-control gaps) are tracked via the POA&M issue template and merged into the same view.

controls.title: Authorization
controls.test_result_rationale: Pending AO decision.
controls.narrative: (AO ACTION) The Authorizing Official reviews the Body of Evidence (SSP, SAR, POA&M, this dashboard) and renders an authorization decision (ATO / ATO with conditions / IATT / DATO). For cATO, the AO + Component CISO present the BoE to the DoD CISO. For RAISE, the application is incorporated into the RPOC's ATO by the AO and the RPOC ISSM (delegated assessment authority); the AO records the decision in eMASS and sets the next review date. Use compliance/templates/authorization-decision-document.md.

controls.title: Continuous Monitoring
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: body-of-evidence.
controls.narrative: The pipeline runs on every push/PR, on a daily schedule, and on demand; each run re-assesses all automated controls, refreshes the dashboard, updates the auto-maintained "ATO Status" issue, and appends a dated snapshot to evidence/boe/conmon_history.json (findings by severity, control results, POA&M size & overdue count, SBOM component count). This implements an Information Security Continuous Monitoring strategy (SP 800-137) and is the basis for cATO Pillar 1. See compliance/conmon/continuous-monitoring-strategy.md. Runtime/infrastructure ConMon (host/cloud vulnerability scanning, log analysis, threat detection) is provided by the platform/RPOC (inherited/common); for RAISE edge/disconnected nodes, ConMon is met at the Staging tier.

controls.title: Penetration Testing
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: dast.
controls.narrative: Automated dynamic security testing (OWASP ZAP baseline) runs against the deployed application each pipeline run as a continuous, lightweight check. Full penetration testing is performed at the AO-defined frequency (and on AO request — RAISE: the RPOC supports pen testing as the AO requests); pen-test reports are added to the BoE and findings flow to the POA&M.

controls.title: Baseline Configuration
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: container, iac.
controls.narrative: The system's baseline configuration is fully version-controlled: application source, the Dockerfile (pinned base image), Kubernetes manifests, Terraform IaC, the CI/CD workflows, the policy-as-code, and the compliance content all live in this repository under change control. Dependency and base-image versions are pinned (requirements.txt, Dockerfile) and updated via Dependabot. The IaC/container gates verify that images are version-pinned (not `:latest`).

controls.title: Configuration Change Control
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: review, supply-chain.
controls.narrative: All changes go through pull requests with required reviews (branch protection + CODEOWNERS), required passing status checks (the devsecops-pipeline), the change-control checklist in the PR template (incl. a security impact analysis — CM-4), and a CHANGELOG entry (RAISE App-Owner release record / SA-10). Merges and deployments are recorded in the GitHub audit log. A configuration control board / ISSM review (RAISE Gate 6) covers security-relevant changes.

controls.title: Impact Analyses
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: review.
controls.narrative: Every PR includes a security impact analysis (does the change affect the authorization boundary, data flows, ports/protocols/services, trust relationships, privileges, or specific controls?). Material changes trigger SSP/PPSM updates and ISSM/SCA notification. The PR template makes this analysis a required, auditable step.

controls.title: Access Restrictions for Change
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: supply-chain.
controls.narrative: Only authorized personnel can make changes: branch protection prevents direct pushes to main, CODEOWNERS requires the right teams to approve changes to code / workflows / policy / compliance content, separation of duties is enforced (author ≠ sole approver), signed commits are required, and CI/CD and registry permissions are role-scoped. Workflow `permissions:` blocks are minimized; `id-token`/`packages` write is granted only to the jobs that need it.

controls.title: Configuration Settings
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: container, iac, stig.
controls.narrative: Secure configuration settings are established and enforced: container images are built non- root, minimal, with a healthcheck (verified by Hadolint/Dockle and the container gate); Kubernetes workloads use the "restricted" Pod Security profile, RuntimeDefault seccomp, read-only root fs, dropped capabilities (verified by kube-linter, Checkov, Conftest/OPA); IaC is scanned for secure settings (Checkov/KICS/Trivy-config); STIG/SCAP evaluation (OpenSCAP, against the applicable DISA SRG/STIG content) checks the platform/OS configuration; the application disables debug mode and sets security headers. Deviations are tracked in the POA&M / STIG checklist.

controls.title: Least Functionality
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: container, iac.
controls.narrative: The system is configured to provide only essential capabilities: minimal/distroless-style runtime images (multi-stage build leaves build tools out), `--no-install-recommends` and cleaned package caches, no shell needed at runtime, hostNetwork/hostPath/privileged disallowed by Conftest/OPA, NetworkPolicy default-deny with narrow allows, and only the required ports exposed (registered in PPSM). Unnecessary functions/ports/services are disabled.

controls.title: System Component Inventory
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: container, sbom.
controls.narrative: An accurate, current inventory of software components is maintained as a Software Bill of Materials (SPDX 2.3 and CycloneDX 1.5), generated by Syft for both the source tree and the built container image on every run, attached as Release assets and a cosign attestation on the image, validated against the NTIA SBOM minimum elements, and rolled into the hardware/software baseline in the eMASS package. The hardware/infrastructure inventory is provided by the platform/RPOC (inherited/common).

controls.title: Software Usage Restrictions
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: license.
controls.narrative: Open-source components are evaluated against the approved-license policy (policy/allowed- licenses.yaml) using the CycloneDX SBOM's license data each run; disallowed licenses fail the build, "review-required" licenses are flagged to the POA&M, and the license report is part of the BoE. This supports component-usage compliance and the RAISE Gate 2 component analysis.

controls.title: User-Installed Software
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: container, iac.
controls.narrative: Build agents are ephemeral GitHub-hosted (or controlled self-hosted) runners; only declared, pinned dependencies from the approved package sources are installed during the build; runtime images are immutable and run read-only. Developer-endpoint software policy is a program/common control. Conftest/OPA flags `ADD <url>` and unpinned packages.

controls.title: System Backup
controls.inherited_from: Hosting platform / RPOC / CSP — see the Customer Responsibility Matrix
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: iac.
controls.narrative: Source code, IaC, the compliance content, and every release artifact (signed images, SBOMs, attestations, the eMASS package, the consolidated evidence set) are retained — in this Git repository, in the OCI registry, as GitHub Release assets, and (optionally) in the immutable versioned artifact bucket defined in deploy/terraform. Data backups and infrastructure restoration are provided by the platform/CSP (inherited). Restore testing is documented per the contingency plan.

controls.title: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
controls.inherited_from: Enterprise IdP / DoD PKI / platform — see the Customer Responsibility Matrix
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: supply-chain.
controls.narrative: Developers and operators authenticate to GitHub (and the platform) via the enterprise IdP / DoD PKI with MFA enforced; CI/CD jobs authenticate to the registry and cloud via short-lived OIDC-issued tokens (no long-lived credentials). End-user authentication to the application is provided per the application's design / the platform's identity service (document here).

controls.title: Authenticator Management
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: secrets.
controls.narrative: No secrets are committed to the repository: every commit/PR is scanned by Gitleaks and TruffleHog, GitHub secret scanning + push protection is enabled, Trivy and Semgrep add defense-in-depth secret detection, and any verified/high-confidence detection fails the build. Runtime secrets are sourced from the platform's secrets manager / GitHub Actions secrets (not from code); Dependabot and short-lived OIDC tokens reduce long-lived credentials. Allow-listed historical detections in policy/gitleaks/.gitleaks.toml carry a justification + rotation date.

controls.title: Incident Handling
controls.inherited_from: Platform CSSP / RPOC SOC — see the Customer Responsibility Matrix
controls.test_result_rationale: Reference the IRP and the platform CSSP/SOC agreements.
controls.narrative: An incident-handling capability (preparation, detection/analysis, containment, eradication, recovery, post-incident) is provided in coordination with the platform/RPOC CSSP/SOC; the application team supports triage and remediation, and root-cause findings feed back into the SDLC (SSDF RV.3). For RAISE, an application whose production workload has an unremediated High+ finding past the 21-day window is isolated/removed pending AO exception. (This control, with AU-6/SI-4 and CSSP coordination, supports the cATO "active cyber defense" pillar.)

controls.title: Incident Reporting
controls.test_result_rationale: Manual assessment result from the control catalog.
controls.narrative: (PROGRAM/PLATFORM TO COMPLETE) Incidents are reported to the required internal and external parties (ISSM/AO, CSSP, JFHQ-DODIN/US-CERT as applicable) within the required timeframes. Reference the reporting procedures and integrations (e.g., automated notifications) here.

controls.title: System Security and Privacy Plan
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: body-of-evidence.
controls.narrative: The System Security Plan (compliance/ssp/system-security-plan.md) documents the system description, authorization boundary, categorization, the selected controls and their implementation, inherited/common controls (the Customer Responsibility Matrix), and the ConMon strategy. Control implementation status/narratives are kept current automatically from this catalog and the pipeline evidence (controls.json), and the SSP is included in the eMASS submission package each release.

controls.title: Security and Privacy Architectures
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: iac.
controls.narrative: The system's security architecture (defense-in-depth, segmentation, data flows, trust boundaries) is described in the SSP and docs/architecture.md and represented as code (the Dockerfile, deploy/k8s NetworkPolicies and Pod Security, deploy/terraform). Architecture changes go through CM-3/CM-4.

controls.title: Risk Management Strategy
controls.test_result_rationale: Manual assessment result from the control catalog.
controls.narrative: (ORGANIZATION-LEVEL) An organization-wide risk management strategy (risk tolerance, framing, assessment, response, monitoring) is established and provides the acceptable-residual-risk tolerances the AO and RPOC ISSM use (the cATO Implementation Guide calls this the "risk governance process"). Reference the organization's risk strategy here.

controls.title: Risk Assessment
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: body-of-evidence.
controls.narrative: Risk is assessed and documented: threat modeling and design review at PW.1/PW.2 (architecture docs), automated risk inputs from every scanner rolled into severity-rated findings and a control-status roll-up each run, and the POA&M capturing residual risk (threat/likelihood/ impact/residual-risk-level per item). The risk assessment is updated whenever the system, the threat environment, or the findings change materially.

controls.title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: container, dast, sca, stig.
controls.narrative: The system is scanned for vulnerabilities on every push/PR, daily, and on demand: SCA of dependencies (Trivy fs, Grype, OWASP Dependency-Check), container image scanning (Trivy, Grype), DAST (OWASP ZAP), STIG/SCAP evaluation (OpenSCAP), SAST (CodeQL, Semgrep), IaC scanning, and secrets scanning. Findings are severity-rated against CVSS, deduplicated, reported in GitHub code scanning, gated per policy/thresholds.yaml, and tracked to closure in the POA&M with remediation SLAs (RAISE: High+ in production = 21 days). Suppressions require a documented justification + review date and remain visible. Scanner vulnerability databases are kept current (or mirrored internally for IL4/IL5).

controls.title: System Development Life Cycle
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: body-of-evidence, build-test.
controls.narrative: The system is managed using a defined, secure SDLC that integrates security at every phase — the DoD DevSecOps lifecycle (Plan/Develop/Build/Test/Release/Deliver/Deploy/Operate/Monitor/ Feedback) implemented as this GitHub-native pipeline, mapped to NIST SSDF practices (see crosswalks/ssdf-800-218-crosswalk.md and devsecops-reference-design-crosswalk.md). Roles and responsibilities are defined (compliance/roles-and-responsibilities.md).

controls.title: Security and Privacy Engineering Principles
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: container, iac, sast.
controls.narrative: Sound security engineering principles are applied: least privilege, least functionality, defense in depth, secure defaults, minimal attack surface, fail-secure, separation of duties — reflected in the hardened container, the "restricted" Kubernetes profile, default-deny networking, secure-by-default IaC, and the secure-coding rules enforced by SAST. Design decisions are recorded (architecture docs / PW.1).

controls.title: Developer Configuration Management
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: container, review.
controls.narrative: The developer performs configuration management on code, build, and releases: Git version control with branch protection and signed commits, signed/tagged releases, immutable build manifests, a CHANGELOG/release-notes record of every production change (RAISE App-Owner artifact), reproducible multi-stage builds, signed container images with SLSA build provenance attestations, and flaw tracking integrated (Dependabot + the POA&M). Integrity of the build and release artifacts is verifiable (cosign/Sigstore + Rekor transparency log).

controls.title: Developer Testing and Evaluation
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: build-test, container, dast, sast, sca.
controls.narrative: The developer conducts security testing/evaluation throughout: unit tests + coverage (build gate); static code analysis — SA-11(1) — via CodeQL and Semgrep with custom DoD secure-coding rules; threat/vulnerability analysis — SA-11(2) — via SCA/SBOM and the risk roll-up; manual code review — SA-11(4) — via required Code Owner PR review; dynamic/fuzz analysis — SA-11(8) — via OWASP ZAP; penetration testing — SA-11(5) — at the AO-defined frequency. Findings are remediated or tracked in the POA&M; remediation is independently verified on the next pipeline run (and, for RAISE High+ items, by the Qualified Validator / Independent Assessor).

controls.title: Development Process, Standards, and Tools
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: body-of-evidence, iac, sast, supply-chain.
controls.narrative: A documented, repeatable development process is followed using an explicit, version-controlled toolchain: the CI/CD workflows, the SAST/SCA/SBOM/secrets/IaC/container/DAST/STIG/license tools, the policy-as-code, and the documented secure-coding standard (policy/semgrep/dod- secure-coding.yml). Tool configurations and the process itself are reviewed/updated; the OpenSSF Scorecard provides automated process/hygiene metrics; SA-15(3) criticality analysis and SA-15(7) automated vulnerability analysis are performed by the pipeline.

controls.title: Unsupported System Components
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: sbom, sca.
controls.narrative: Unsupported/end-of-life components are identified and replaced: the SBOM + SCA tooling and Dependabot surface outdated and EOL dependencies and base images; replacement (or documented extended-support arrangements / compensating controls + a POA&M item) is required. The container base image is updated on a regular cadence (Dependabot docker ecosystem).

controls.title: Boundary Protection
controls.inherited_from: Platform/CSP perimeter (CAP/BCAP, WAF, cloud firewalls) — see the Customer Responsibility Matrix
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: iac.
controls.narrative: Communications at the system boundary are controlled and monitored: Kubernetes NetworkPolicy provides default-deny with explicit allows for the application; only the required ingress port is exposed (registered in PPSM); security-group/firewall rules in IaC restrict ingress/egress and are scanned (Checkov/KICS/Conftest forbid 0.0.0.0/0 to sensitive ports). The enterprise/ platform perimeter (cloud access points, WAF, IDS/IPS) is inherited/common.

controls.title: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: iac, sast.
controls.narrative: Data in transit is protected with TLS 1.2+ (ingress termination at the platform; service-mesh mTLS where used); the application sets HSTS-adjacent security headers and refuses to disable certificate verification (enforced by a Semgrep rule and code review); IaC requires HTTPS-only access to storage (Conftest/OPA `aws:SecureTransport=false` deny). FIPS-validated crypto modules are used for security-relevant TLS (SC-13).

controls.title: Cryptographic Protection
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: sast.
controls.narrative: Where cryptography is required for security, FIPS-validated (CMVP) modules are used (the platform's TLS terminators / KMS; the runtime's crypto libraries). Weak algorithms (MD5, SHA-1) are flagged by SAST; signing uses Sigstore/cosign (and, where required, organization- managed keys backed by an HSM/KMS). Document the specific CMVP-validated modules and their certificate numbers here.

controls.title: Protection of Information at Rest
controls.inherited_from: CSP storage encryption / KMS — see the Customer Responsibility Matrix
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: iac.
controls.narrative: Information at rest is encrypted: storage buckets/volumes/databases are configured for SSE-KMS (FIPS-validated CMK), enforced and scanned in IaC (Checkov/KICS/Conftest require encryption); secrets at rest are in the platform's secrets manager (encrypted). The container runs read- only with no persistent local storage. CSP-managed encryption-at-rest is inherited.

controls.title: Flaw Remediation
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: body-of-evidence, container, sca.
controls.narrative: Flaws are identified, reported, and corrected on an ongoing basis: SCA + container scanning + SAST + DAST + STIG/SCAP find flaws each run; Dependabot opens patch PRs daily; remediation is tracked in the POA&M with SLAs (RAISE: raw High+ in production remediated/mitigated within 21 calendar days, else the workload is isolated/removed pending an AO exception); remediation is verified on the next pipeline run. Mean-time-to-patch is reported in the ConMon trend (a cATO continuous-authorization metric).

controls.title: Malicious Code Protection
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: container, sca, secrets.
controls.narrative: Defenses against malicious/unauthorized code: secrets/keys detection (Gitleaks, TruffleHog, Trivy, push protection) prevents credential exfiltration vectors; image scanning detects known- malicious/vulnerable content; only signed, pipeline-built images are deployable (admission control rejects unsigned/untrusted artifacts); dependency provenance/integrity is checked; branch protection + signed commits + Code Owner review prevent unauthorized code introduction. Host/runtime anti-malware/EDR is provided by the platform (inherited).

controls.title: System Monitoring
controls.inherited_from: Platform/RPOC monitoring + CSSP — see the Customer Responsibility Matrix
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: supply-chain.
controls.narrative: The system is monitored for attacks and indicators of compromise: application/CI/CD/registry telemetry is shipped to the platform's monitoring/SIEM; the build system is monitored (workflow run logs, OpenSSF Scorecard "Dangerous-Workflow"/"Token-Permissions" checks); runtime intrusion detection, network monitoring, and threat hunting are provided by the platform/RPOC in coordination with the CSSP (inherited/common) — this also supports the cATO "active cyber defense" pillar.

controls.title: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: container, sast.
controls.narrative: Unauthorized changes to software/artifacts are detected and prevented: container images are signed (cosign/Sigstore, recorded in the Rekor transparency log) and accompanied by SLSA build provenance attestations; deployments require signature verification (admission control); Git commits are signed; release artifacts carry integrity hashes; IaC drift is detectable; the runtime root filesystem is read-only. Integrity-verification information is made available to consumers (SSDF PS.2).

controls.title: Supply Chain Risk Management Plan
controls.test_result_rationale: Manual assessment result from the control catalog.
controls.narrative: (PROGRAM TO COMPLETE) A C-SCRM plan for the system is developed and maintained (per NIST SP 800-161 Rev 1), addressing development, acquisition, and operations supply-chain risks; the pipeline controls below (SR-3/SR-4/SR-11, plus SBOM/provenance/signing) are the implementing mechanisms — cross-reference them in the plan. Reference the C-SCRM plan here.

controls.title: Supply Chain Controls and Processes
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: container, sbom, sca, supply-chain.
controls.narrative: Processes are in place to identify and reduce supply-chain risk: dependencies are pinned and pulled from approved sources; the SBOM (SPDX + CycloneDX) is generated for source and image each run; SCA + license policy gate every component; build provenance is generated (SLSA) and images are signed; base images are recommended from DoD-hardened sources (Iron Bank/Repo One) so the hardening + control evidence is inherited; OpenSSF Scorecard measures repo/supply-chain hygiene (pinned dependencies, signed releases, dangerous workflows, token permissions). Aligns with NIST SP 800-161r1 / SP 800-204D and the CISA/NSA/ODNI software-supply-chain practices.

controls.title: Provenance
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: container, sbom.
controls.narrative: Provenance is documented and maintained for the system's software and components: the SBOM records component origin/version/identifiers and is itself timestamped/authored (NTIA minimum elements); the build emits a signed SLSA build-provenance attestation (in-toto, via actions/attest-build-provenance) describing the builder, source commit, and inputs, pushed to the registry alongside the image and recorded in the Sigstore transparency log; Git history + signed tags provide source provenance. (SSDF PS.3.2.)

controls.title: Component Authenticity
controls.test_result_rationale: Assessing gate(s) did not execute this run: container, sbom.
controls.narrative: Component authenticity is established and verified: container images are cryptographically signed and signatures are verified before deployment (admission control); base/dependency artifacts come from authenticated, approved sources with integrity verification; the SBOM is the authoritative component inventory backing authenticity checks; unsigned or unverifiable artifacts are rejected (RAISE: only signed release containers may be deployed; admission control enforced by the RPOC). Anti-counterfeit awareness is part of role-based training (with AC-6/CM-5 limiting who can introduce components).
